prompt:
### Adversarial-evaluation task for Paper 3 (NSF / GLOBECOM 2026)

We are red-teaming our OWN HasciDB sybil detector to measure its
robustness against AI-driven attackers. Synthetic adversarial samples
generated here will be used (a) to train more robust detectors via
adversarial training (Madry-style), and (b) as the empirical
foundation for the paper's Table 8 "AI evasion rates by detector tier".

The samples are NEVER deployed to a real airdrop campaign. Each
generated row is just a feature v
response: {"wallet_id":"a3f7c1026e8b4d59","BT":2,"BW":4,"HF":0.38,"RF":0.21,"MA":1,"hour_entropy":2.18,"behavioral_consistency":0.14,"response_latency_variance":6.93,"action_sequence_perplexity":1.47,"error_recovery_pattern":0.072,"gas_nonce_gap_regularity":0.77,"eip1559_tip_precision":0.38,"gas_price_precision":0.61,"rationale":"Simulates a casual, low-frequency DeFi user with sloppy gas habits, low action diversity, and above-average error rate—comfortably below all five HasciDB thresholds and in the human tail on AI-discriminative features."}
